# framejs.io

> Run, share, and embed JavaScript apps in a single URL.

## What is framejs.io?

framejs.io is an open-source browser-based JavaScript runtime. Code is encoded
directly into the URL — no server, no account, no build step. Write ES6 modules
with top-level await, import npm packages from CDN, connect inputs/outputs
between components, and share via a single link.

## Who is it for?

- Developers wanting instant shareable JavaScript apps
- Data scientists embedding interactive visualizations in Jupyter or marimo
  notebooks
- AI/LLM users generating code from natural language prompts
- Teams building embeddable dashboards and internal tools

## Key links

- Homepage: https://framejs.io
- Documentation: https://framejs.io/docs/
- Examples: https://framejs.io/docs/examples/
- AI / LLM setup: https://framejs.io/docs/ai/setup
- LLM code prompt (for generating code): https://framejs.io/llms-prompt.md
- GitHub: https://github.com/metapages/metaframe-js
- Jupyter integration: https://framejs.io/docs/integrations/jupyter
- marimo integration: https://framejs.io/docs/integrations/marimo

## How it works

1. JavaScript is encoded into the URL hash: `https://framejs.io/#?js=<base64>`
2. The browser loads and executes the code as an ES6 module
3. Short URLs (`/j/<sha256>`) store the hash params in S3 for cleaner sharing
4. The editor opens in-browser — no external tools needed
5. Metaframes can be connected together via inputs/outputs in a metapage

## Technical details

- Runtime: ES6 modules with top-level await, "use strict" added automatically
- Globals: `root` (display div), `setOutput()`, `setOutputs()`, `log()`,
  `logStdout()`, `logStderr()`
- Exports: `onInputs(inputs)`, `onResize(width, height)`, `cleanup()`
- CDN imports: Use jsdelivr (+esm), esm.sh, unpkg, etc.
- State persistence: Encoded in URL hash — fully portable
- No cookies, no tracking, no login required
